subject: [PATCH v5 2/3] cxl/core/region: move dax region device logic into region_dax.c

reviews.author: Anisa Su
reviews.summary: Reviewer Anisa Su noted that the patch is missing an assignment to cxlr->cxlr_dax, which causes a segmentation fault; she requested the addition of cxlr->cxlr_dax = cxlr_dax;
reviews.raw_body:
cxlr->cxlr_dax = cxlr_dax;

Running into segfaults without this ^

reviews.author: Dave Jiang
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested a separate fix-up patch to address the changes made in this patch, without specifying what exactly needs fixing.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you pls create a fixes patch?

DJ

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the patch was supposed to be a non-functional change, implying that the issue is not about functionality but rather about the correctness of the code movement.
reviews.raw_body:
This should have been a purely non-functional change (the code is just
moving from A to B without changes).

That would mean the original code should run into this too unless I
mucked up the move.

~Gregory

2026-03-27.patch_summary: This patch moves the CXL DAX region device logic from the overloaded region.c file into a new region_dax.c file, allowing for additional dax-driver integration paths without further dirtying the core region.c logic. The move includes the creation of a new region_dax.c file and modifications to the Makefile, core.h, and region.c files. No functional changes are made as part of this patch.
